items.why: Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.q: How does a harmonic minor scale differ from a natural minor?
items.correct: The seventh degree is raised by a semitone (creating a leading note).
items.misconception: All notes are raised by a semitone.
items.why: Students think the whole scale changes. Only the 7TH is altered to create a leading note, which strengthens cadences in minor keys - producing the characteristic augmented second between 6th and 7th.

items.correct: Three sections: A, contrasting B, and a return to A (ABA).
items.why: Students assume 'three sections' means three different ones. Ternary has only TWO distinct ideas, but A returns - it's the return that makes it ternary, not the section count alone.

items.q: Programmatic music is best described as:
items.correct: Music that tells a story or describes a scene
items.why: It's Music that tells a story or describes a scene. “Music written for computers” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which keyboard instrument strongly suggests Baroque style?
items.why: Correct answer: Harpsichord. “Piano” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many distinct musical traditions are minimally combined to count as 'fusion'?
items.why: The real figure is 2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
